label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking when an ordinary tropical storm is called a hurricane. The options are A through D. Looking at the first paragraph of the provided text: "In the second half of each year, many powerful storms are born in the tropical Atlantic and Caribbean seas. Of these, only about half a dozen becomes the strong, circling winds of 75 miles per hour or more that are called hurricanes..." So here it says that when the storms have winds of 75 mph or more, they are called hurricanes. The key point here is the wind speed. The answer choices include option D which is "When its winds reach 75 miles per hour." That seems to match directly with the text. Let me check the other options to make sure. Option A says when it begins in the Atlantic and Caribbean seas. But the text mentions that many storms are born there, but only some become hurricanes, so the location isn't the determining factor. Option B is when it hits the coastline.
